headline: JPMorgan Urges Caution on Prediction Markets While Allowing Employees to Trade Them
summary: New internal guidelines stop short of a ban, potentially allowing employees to trade on prediction markets involving the company itself.

content.title: JPMorgan Urges Caution on Prediction Markets While Allowing Employees to Trade Them

headline: How The Autotrader Group (LSE:AUTO) Story Is Shifting With Lower Targets And Higher Risk
summary: Autotrader Group is back in focus after its fair value estimate was trimmed from £6.73 to £6.54 per share, alongside fresh price target cuts such as JPMorgan moving to 515 GBp from 630 GBp. These shifts align with a broader, more cautious tone in recent research, where analysts are recalibrating what they are willing to pay for the stock while debating how much execution risk to factor in. The following sections outline how to interpret these changing targets and track the evolving story...

content.title: How The Autotrader Group (LSE:AUTO) Story Is Shifting With Lower Targets And Higher Risk

headline: A Look At Cava Group’s Valuation As Wall Street Upgrades And Expansion Plans Lift Expectations
summary: Recent upgrades from firms including BofA and JPMorgan, along with upbeat earnings estimates and expansion plans, have pushed CAVA Group (CAVA) into the spotlight ahead of its upcoming results. See our latest analysis for CAVA Group. CAVA’s recent 1-day share price return of a 5.63% decline and 7-day share price return of a 9.57% decline contrast with its 90-day share price return of 20.74% and year to date share price return of 39.50%. This suggests that momentum has cooled in the very short...

headline: AT&T to Webcast Fireside Chat with John Stankey at the 2026 J.P. Morgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference on May 19
summary: AT&T (NYSE:T) will webcast a fireside chat with John Stankey, chairman and chief executive officer, AT&T Inc., at the 2026 J.P. Morgan Global Technology, Media and Communications Conference on Tuesday, May 19, 2026. The conversation is scheduled to begin at 8:00 a.m. ET.

headline: Vinson & Elkins: Two Good to Be True? The SEC Proposes Optional Semiannual Reporting
summary: By Robert L. Kimball, Katherine Terrell Frank, Alex Bahn, Jon Solorzano, Alexandra M. Lewis, Chloe Schmergel, Josh Rutenberg, Randy Thomas, and Matt Turk On May 5, 2026, the SEC proposed to provide public companies the option of filing interim reports semiannually on new Form 10-S instead of quarterly on Form 10-Q. The proposal would amend Rules 13a-13 […] The post Vinson & Elkins: Two Good to Be True? The SEC Proposes Optional Semiannual Reporting appeared first on CorpGov.
